title: US chip giant Micron validates growth of China's CXMT and YMTC amid tight memory market
gist: Micron posted record $41.5B fiscal Q3 revenue and guided to $50B on AI-driven memory demand it expects to stay tight beyond 2027, while acknowledging Chinese rivals CXMT and YMTC are rapidly gaining DRAM and NAND share in the conventional (non-HBM) market.

insights.claim: Micron reported fiscal third-quarter revenue of $41.46 billion, up from $23.86 billion the prior quarter and $9.30 billion a year earlier, and guided to record fiscal Q4 revenue of $50 billion plus or minus $1 billion.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A ~4.5x year-over-year revenue jump quantifies the AI memory supercycle and eased fears of an AI bubble.
insights.quote: fiscal third-quarter revenue of US$41.46 billion, up from US$23.86 billion in the previous quarter and US$9.30 billion a year earlier

insights.claim: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra said tight memory conditions are expected to persist beyond calendar 2027 due to AI-driven demand across all segments coupled with structural supply constraints.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A multi-year tight-supply outlook supports sustained memory pricing power and capex; a durable bull thesis for memory names.
insights.quote: We expect tight conditions to persist beyond calendar 2027 as a result of AI-driven demand across all segments coupled with structural supply constraints

insights.claim: Micron said it signed 16 strategic customer agreements expected to improve long-term demand visibility.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Long-term customer agreements de-risk the historically boom-bust memory cycle and support forward revenue visibility.
insights.quote: signed 16 strategic customer agreements that were expected to improve long-term demand visibility

insights.claim: TrendForce estimated conventional DRAM contract prices would rise 58-63% quarter on quarter in Q2 2026, while NAND flash contract prices were forecast to increase 70-75% quarter on quarter.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Double-digit-to-70%+ QoQ price hikes signal an extreme memory shortage benefiting all suppliers, including Chinese entrants.
insights.quote: contract prices for conventional DRAM were expected to rise between 58 and 63 per cent quarter on quarter

insights.claim: DRAM suppliers are shifting wafer fab capacity to HBM and server products while NAND capacity is tilting toward enterprise SSDs used in AI servers, tightening conventional memory supply.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Capacity migration to AI-grade memory is the mechanism creating the conventional-DRAM shortage that Chinese suppliers exploit.
insights.quote: DRAM suppliers were shifting more wafer fab capacity to HBM and server-related products, while NAND capacity was tilting towards enterprise solid state drives
insights.locator: The research firm said DRAM suppliers

insights.claim: Micron chief business officer Sumit Sadana acknowledged CXMT and YMTC have grown in capability and market share, though the overwhelming majority of their output is still sold within China.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Validation from the market leader signals Chinese memory makers are becoming credible competitors outside HBM.
insights.quote: they had both grown in capability and market share over the years, although the "overwhelming majority" of their output was still sold within China

insights.claim: CXMT's global DRAM revenue share rose to 8 percent in Q1 2026 from 3 percent a year earlier, per Counterpoint data.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: A near-tripling of DRAM share in one year quantifies how fast Chinese entrants are taking conventional-memory volume.
insights.quote: the company's global DRAM revenue share rose to 8 per cent in the first quarter this year, up from 3 per cent a year earlier
insights.locator: That pricing cycle has already enhanced

insights.claim: CXMT reported Q1 revenue of 50.8 billion yuan and net profit of 33 billion yuan versus a loss a year earlier, and guided first-half revenue of 110-120 billion yuan with net profit of 66-75 billion yuan.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: CXMT's swing to large profits ahead of its IPO shows the pricing cycle is funding Chinese expansion; watch the listing.
insights.quote: revenue of 50.8 billion yuan (US$7.47 billion) in the first quarter and net profit of 33 billion yuan, compared with a loss a year earlier
insights.locator: Documents from CXMT's planned initial public offering

insights.claim: SemiAnalysis attributed CXMT's earnings surge to conventional DRAM exposure rather than HBM, which it said contributes minimally to CXMT's revenue and earnings.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: CXMT's profitability rides the conventional-DRAM shortage, not premium HBM, so its trajectory is tied to that cycle's persistence.
insights.quote: HBM's contribution to CXMT's revenue and earnings was minimal, attributing the company's recent earnings surge to its exposure to a tight conventional DRAM market
insights.locator: In a June report, SemiAnalysis said

insights.claim: YMTC's global NAND revenue share rose to 13 percent in Q1 2026 from 8 percent a year earlier, per Counterpoint data.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: YMTC's NAND share gains show Chinese storage makers benefiting from AI data-center demand alongside DRAM peers.
insights.quote: its global NAND revenue share rose to 13 per cent in the first quarter of 2026, up from 8 per cent a year earlier
insights.locator: YMTC, China's leading NAND flash maker

insights.claim: Bernstein reported that Chinese memory chipmakers have significantly sped up capacity expansion plans due to robust demand and ample capital.
insights.speaker: SemiWiki
insights.so_what: Accelerated Chinese capacity buildout could pressure conventional-memory pricing later, a risk to the durability of the current tightness.
insights.quote: Chinese memory chipmakers had "significantly sped up" their capacity expansion plans because of robust demand and ample capital
